food_subgroup: Sago tapioca pudding with palm sugar sauce and coconut cream

ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0GBQ is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor for a sago-based pudding. No exact code for Sago Gula Melaka with coconut cream exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Sago Pudding with Gula Melaka is approximately 180g, comprising chilled sago pearls set in coconut milk with a warm gula melaka palm sugar sauce and coconut cream drizzle, as commonly served at Singapore hawker stalls and Peranakan restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Sago Gula Melaka — Health Promotion Board Singapore (Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Sago Gula Melaka. HPB Foodo is the primary approved source for Singapore dishes. Values represent a composite of sago pearls, coconut milk, coconut cream, and gula melaka palm sugar sauce.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — sago pearls (tapioca starch) GI ~66; palm sugar (gula melaka) GI ~35
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~65 is a weighted composite estimate: sago pearls (tapioca starch) GI ~66 per University of Sydney GI Database; gula melaka (palm sugar) GI ~35 per peer-reviewed literature (lower than refined sucrose GI ~65). The composite GI of ~65 reflects the dominant starch contribution of sago pearls. GL per 180g serving = (65 × 32g sugar+starch) / 100 ≈ 19.5. This is classified as medium GI but high GL per serving.

hypertension.guidance: Sago Pudding with Gula Melaka is low in sodium (~16.7mg/100g, ~30mg per serving) and poses no meaningful sodium burden for hypertensive patients; however, the high saturated fat content (~9g per serving from coconut milk and cream) warrants monitoring for long-term cardiovascular LDL effects in patients with concurrent dyslipidaemia.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~19.5 per 180g serving) driven by high-GI sago starch and 32g total sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid Sago Pudding with Gula Melaka due to its high glycemic load of ~19.5 per serving (GI ~65, ~32g sugar per 180g); if consumed, a half-portion (~90–100g, GL ~10–11) is preferable, and it should be paired with a low-GI meal to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~9g per 180g serving, predominantly lauric acid C12:0 from coconut milk and cream)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should consume Sago Pudding with Gula Melaka infrequently due to its high saturated fat content (~5g/100g, ~9g per serving) from coconut milk and cream, which may elevate LDL cholesterol with regular consumption; the high sugar load (~32g per serving) also contributes to hypertriglyceridaemia risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory combination of high saturated fat (~9g) and high sugar (~32g) per serving
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should limit Sago Pudding with Gula Melaka as the combination of high saturated fat (lauric acid C12:0 activates TLR4 pro-inflammatory pathways) and high sugar (promotes postprandial hyperglycaemia and systemic inflammation) is unfavourable; occasional half-portions (~100g) within an otherwise anti-inflammatory dietary pattern are acceptable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sago Pudding with Gula Melaka (Sago Gula Melaka) is a classic Peranakan and Malay dessert widely served at Singapore hawker stalls and Peranakan restaurants such as Blue Ginger, Candlenut, and National Kitchen. It is made from small, translucent sago pearls (derived from tapioca starch) that are boiled until chewy, then set in a mould or chilled, and served with a generous drizzle of rich coconut milk or coconut cream and a warm, thick dark syrup made from gula melaka (palm sugar). The texture is soft, gelatinous, and slightly chewy, with a neutral starchy base that contrasts with the deep caramel-like sweetness of the palm sugar sauce and the rich, slightly savoury creaminess of the coconut milk. Nutritionally, per 180g serving: approximately 300 kcal, 1g protein, 10g fat (~9g saturated fat from coconut milk/cream), 32g carbohydrate (~32g sugar), 1.1g dietary fibre, and ~30mg sodium. GI ~65 (medium), GL ~19.5 (high per serving). Gula melaka has a lower GI (~35) than refined sucrose (~65), but the total sugar load of 32g per serving means the glycaemic impact remains high. Sago pearls are essentially pure tapioca starch with negligible protein and fibre. The dish is vegetarian, vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is not suitable for patients with Type 2 Diabetes due to high GL, and should be consumed infrequently by those with cardiovascular disease or rheumatoid arthritis due to high saturated fat. Source: HPB Singapore Foodo. Confidence: high.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo, the primary approved source for Singapore dishes. GI estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for sago pearls (tapioca starch GI ~66) and peer-reviewed literature for gula melaka (palm sugar GI ~35). The composite GI of ~65 is well-supported. All core macros are complete and internally consistent. Confidence is high.
notes: Per-serving values (180g): energy ~300 kcal, protein ~1g, fat ~10g (saturated ~9g), carbohydrate ~32g, sugar ~32g, dietary fibre ~1.1g, sodium ~30mg. The input record contained two conflicting default portions (120g and 180g); the 180g portion is adopted as the canonical serving size per the verified HPB source and the food name description. Sugar equals total carbohydrate per 100g in the source data, reflecting that sago pearls contribute pure starch (which hydrolyses to glucose) and gula melaka contributes sucrose; the sugar_g field therefore equals carbohydrate_g at the per-100g level. Cholesterol is confirmed zero (plant-based ingredients only). Vitamin B12 is confirmed zero (no animal-derived ingredients). Vitamin D is confirmed zero (no UV-exposed or fortified ingredients). Trans fat is confirmed zero per HPB source. Vitamin K is reported as 0.0 per enriched source (confidence 0.9). The FoodEx2 code A0GBQ is used as the closest parent match; no exact FoodEx2 code exists for this specific preparation. No FoodOn URI is available for Sago Gula Melaka. Water_g is -1.0 as it is not reported by the source.
